steps.title: Track the delivery problem
steps.content: Consumers expect faster delivery, but companies struggle with last-mile logistics. Even proposed innovations, such as sidewalk delivery robots, create additional complications.

steps.title: Infer the feasibility limit
steps.content: B shifts to delivery volume, C merely says faster delivery would be beneficial, and D contradicts the fact that companies are already trying solutions. The repeated obstacles suggest expectations may exceed what companies can viably provide. The answer is A.
